channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: What to know about El Salvador's mega-prison after Trump deal to send people there
item.description: SAN SALVADOR, El Salvador (AP) — The Trump administration and the president of El Salvador said Monday that they'd struck a deal allowing the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MARCOS ALEMÁN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Baltimore mayor fights back against attacks on DEI initiatives with ‘Definitely Earned It’ campaign
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump's attack on diversity, equity and inclusion programs have led military and government agencies — along with some private companies — to roll back observances of diversity and celebrations of Black History Month, which goes through February.
item.dc:creator: MATT BROWN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Inside the operations that took captive 2 North Korean soldiers fighting Ukraine
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — When Ukrainian soldiers captured two North Korean prisoners of war last month, it provided the first undeniable proof of Pyongyang’s direct involvement in the war against Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Mmm, that looks yummy! The colors we see make a difference in the food we eat
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — You know you've said it. We all have. “Mmm, that looks so delicious — I want to try some!” That's because when it comes to what we eat, it's not just a matter of taste.
item.dc:creator: DEEPTI HAJELA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: A timeline of last week's air disaster in Washington, the deadliest in the US since 2001
item.description: Almost a week after a helicopter and airplane collision in Washington killed 67 people, investigators are still at work as salvage crews pull out chunks of aircraft.
item.dc:creator: Jesse Bedayn, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Gene Barge, renowned sax man and producer known as 'Daddy G,' dies at 98
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Gene “Daddy G” Barge, an admired and durable saxophone player, songwriter and producer who worked on hits by Natalie Cole, oversaw recordings by Muddy Waters, performed with the Rolling Stones and helped inspire the dance classic “Quarter to Three,” has died. He was 98.
item.dc:creator: HILLEL ITALIE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: USAID direct hires put on leave worldwide, except those deemed essential
item.description: The Trump administration is placing U.S. Agency for International Development direct-hire staffers around the world on leave, except those deemed essential.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: China hits U.S. with retaliatory tariffs
item.description: China hit the U.S. back with retaliatory tariffs across the board over President Trump’s enforcement of tariffs on Chinese goods that went into effect on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KTLA 5

item.title: Making climate-friendly lifestyle choices isn't always easy. India learned the hard way
item.description: BENGALURU, India (AP) — For nearly four years, India's government pushed an initiative to get people to think about how to make lifestyle choices that pollute less, like cycling instead of driving or using less plastic.
item.dc:creator: SIBI ARASU, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Man accused of fencing stolen items linked to theft at star athlete's Ohio home, prosecutors say
item.description: One of two men accused of fencing luxury goods stolen from homes across the country has been linked to a theft at the residence of a “high-profile athlete in Ohio" on the same day that Cincinnati Bengals quarterback Joe Burrow's home was broken into, federal authorities said in court documents relea
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Latest: Trump says he wants US to take charge of Gaza Strip at press conference with Netanyahu
item.description: President Donald Trump said Tuesday that he wants the U.S. to take ownership of the Gaza Strip and redevelop it after Palestinians are resettled elsewhere. He made the remarks in a joint press conference with Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Senate confirms Pam Bondi to lead Justice Department
item.description: The Senate on Wednesday voted to confirm Pam Bondi as the next attorney general, giving her control of a Justice Department embroiled in controversy. The vote was 54-46. Sen. John Fetterman (D-Pa.) joined all Republicans in voting in support of Bondi.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, WFLA, NBC

item.title: FBI agents sue over Justice Dept. effort to ID employees involved in Trump-related investigations
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — FBI agents who participated in investigations related to President Donald Trump have sued over Justice Department efforts to develop a list of employees involved in those inquiries that they fear could be a precursor to mass firings.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER and ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: VIDEO: 2 men facing charges following courtroom brawl in Albuquerque
item.description: ALBUQUERQUE, N.M. (KRQE) – A routine court hearing devolved into chaos when two people in the gallery rushed at the defendant and ended up starting a brawl. Now, those two men are facing charges. It happened at a district court hearing Friday morning for murder suspect Alexander Ortiz.
item.dc:creator: Chandler Farnsworth, KRON4

item.title: Treasury tells Congress that DOGE has ‘Read Only’ access to payment systems
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A Treasury Department official wrote a letter Tuesday to federal lawmakers saying that a tech executive working with Elon Musk's Department of Government Efficiency will have “read-only access” to the government's payment system.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN & JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Officials confirm all 67 victims in DC midair crash recovered from Potomac River
item.description: Officials confirmed Tuesday that all 67 victims in the DC midair crash have been recovered from the Potomac River, with 66 bodies positively identified.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Designs unveiled for replacement of Baltimore bridge almost a year after deadly collapse
item.description: SPARROWS POINT, Md. (AP) — Almost a year after the deadly collapse of Baltimore’s Francis Scott Key Bridge, Maryland officials on Tuesday unveiled their designs for its replacement, which will be taller and better protected against ship strikes.
item.dc:creator: LEA SKENE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: The Oregon Zoo welcomes a new baby elephant
item.description: PORTLAND, Ore. (AP) — The Oregon Zoo in Portland has welcomed its newest addition, a baby elephant. Thirty-year-old Asian elephant Rose-Tu gave birth on Saturday after 20 months of pregnancy, the zoo said in a news release.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump to sign executive order banning transgender athletes from women’s sports
item.description: President Donald Trump is expected to sign an executive order Wednesday banning transgender athletes from competing in girls’ and women’s sports, the latest in a string of executive actions from the new administration to take aim at LGBTQ rights.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: What did US get from deals to pause tariffs on Canada and Mexico? Not that much, observers say
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump’s threatened tariffs against Canada and Mexico — now on hold for a month — risked blowing up North America’s economy.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, MARÍA VERZA and ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Agents sue FBI, DOJ amid fears of retaliation over work on Jan. 6 cases
item.description: The FBI and the Department of Justice (DOJ) are being sued by 16 current agents who say their review of those who worked on the cases of some 1,500 Jan. 6 defendants is a preparation to retaliate against agents.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Musk: Trump 'will succeed' in dismantling Education Department
item.description: “Reagan campaigned on ending the federal Dept of Education, which was created by Carter in 1979, but it was bigger when Reagan left office than when he started!” Musk said.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: About 10 killed in shooting at adult education center in Sweden
item.description: Swedish police said that about 10 people, including the gunman, were killed during a shooting Tuesday at an adult education center. But a final death toll and a conclusive number of wounded hadn't yet been determined.
item.dc:creator: STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: China hits US with retaliatory tariffs
item.description: China hit the U.S. back with retaliatory tariffs across the board over President Trump’s enforcement of tariffs on Chinese goods that went into effect on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WGN 9

item.title: Trump wants rare earth elements from Ukraine in exchange for military aid
item.description: “We're looking to do a deal with Ukraine, where they're going to secure what we're giving them with their rare earth and other things,” he told reporters.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, KRON4

item.title: LA wildfires: Famed Pacific Coast Highway reopens nearly 1 month after disaster
item.description: The Pacific Coast Highway in Malibu, California, partially reopened on Monday, for the first time since fires tore through neighborhoods, destroying homes and businesses.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Duffy tells DOT to prioritize areas with high birth rates
item.description: Transportation Secretary Sean Duffy in a memo told the Department of Transportation (DOT) to prioritize communities that have higher birth and marriage rates.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KRON4

item.title: Tariff threats take aim at fentanyl trafficking. Here's how the drug reaches the US
item.description: President Donald Trump's plan to impose tariffs on goods from Mexico, Canada and China is partly aimed at combating the illicit flow of fentanyl into the U.S., where the opioid is blamed for some 70,000 overdose deaths annually. Mexico agreed Monday to send 10,000 troops to the U.S.
item.dc:creator: JESSE BEDAYN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Army identifies 3rd soldier killed in DC crash
item.description: The U.S. Army on Saturday identified the third soldier who died last week during a training mission that ended in a midair crash between an Army Black Hawk helicopter and an American Airlines regional jet near Ronald Reagan Washington National Airport.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Chase Elliott wins Clash at Bowman Gray and sets sights on following it with Daytona 500 victory
item.description: NASCAR returned to its roots with a fight-free preseason exhibition at Bowman Gray Stadium and a popular victory with Chase Elliott winning The Clash on Sunday night.
item.dc:creator: JENNA FRYER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: USAID security leaders on leave after trying to keep Musk's DOGE from classified info: officials
item.description: The two USAID security officials believed themselves legally obligated to deny access to Musk's DOGE crew since it lacked high-enough security clearance to access the information.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Will La Niña be a 'bust' this winter? Meteorologist explains why it may feel like it
item.description: After months of teasing, a La Niña winter has finally started – but we don't blame you if you haven't noticed.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, ABC 4

item.title: Grammy Awards: How to watch the ceremony, red carpet arrivals, interviews
item.description: The 67th annual Grammy Awards are set to recognize some of the most talented performers and biggest hits in pop music on Sunday, with different ways to watch the festivities.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Ohtani's return to pitching could come in May for World Series champion Dodgers
item.description:
Shohei Ohtani pitching for the Los Angeles Dodgers in May is "about right,” manager Dave Roberts said Saturday at the team's annual fan fest.
He reiterated that Ohtani won't pitch in the season-opening Japan Series against the Chicago Cubs on March 18-19 in Tokyo.
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: An air ambulance has crashed in Philadelphia. What to know about air medical transport
item.description: A medical transport jet carrying a child patient, her mother and four others slammed into a Philadelphia neighborhood and exploded in flames.
item.dc:creator: AUDREY McAVOY, Associated Press, ABC 4
